verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (HSP70-1/HSP72) is canonically a cytosolic chaperone with no TM domain, GPI anchor, or intrinsic surface-anchoring mechanism. However, well-documented cell-state-induced surface translocation occurs in multiple contexts: (1) heat shock and other proteotoxic stresses drive HSP70 externalization to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane, where it is detected by surface biotinylation and flow cytometry; (2) oncogenic transformation leads to robust cancer-cell ecto-HSP70 display — tumor cells constitutively present HSP70 on their outer PM face, a finding exploited by NK-cell–activating immunotherapeutic programs (e.g., cmHsp70.1 antibody, HSP70-targeted CAR-NK); (3) immunogenic/apoptotic cell death also releases and exposes HSP70 on the cell surface. The cancer-ecto pool and stress-induced PM pool are the surface story here, qualifying as contextual/cell_state_induced. No stable TM anchor or GPI; baseline localization is cytosolic.
